title: Harness Updating Is Not Harness Benefit: Disentangling Evolution Capabilities in Self-Evolving LLM Agents

C1.1.text: The paper's central methodological contribution is an evaluation framework (cross-pairing matrix) rather than a new algorithm or method.

sources.text_snippet: evaluation of these methods still asks an end-to-end question... hides the source of improvement
capability metrics definitions

C2.1.text: The paper correctly identifies the evaluation gap in self-evolution work but misses relevant citation on capability decomposition methodology.

sources.text_snippet: entire section on harness engineering and self-evolution

C3.1.text: Using max rather than mean for Δ_benefit reflects an 'upper-bound capability' lens, not an 'expected benefit' lens.

sources.text_snippet: Harness-benefit Capability... defined as the maximum pairwise gain across a fixed anchor evolver set

C5.1.text: Harness-updating gain across 7 evolvers spans at most 3.1pp on any benchmark, confirming flatness in base capability.

sources.text_snippet: Observation 1: Harness-updating is flat in base capability
Full evolver-side matrix

C5.2.text: Post-evolution performance is dominated by the task-solving agent's base capability, not evolver identity. Extreme pairings show strong agents lead by 18.6-35.2pp.

sources.text_snippet: Observation 2: Post-evolution score is dominated by models' base capability
Extreme agent-evolver pairings

C6.1.text: Δ_benefit follows an inverted-U shape: mid-tier models benefit most, confirmed on SWE and MCP (cleanest) and SB (noisier but not contradicting).

sources.text_snippet: Observation 1: Δ_benefit is non-monotonic in base capability
Base pass rate and Δ_benefit per agent per benchmark

C7.1.text: Two failure modes explain weak-tier low Δ_benefit: harness activation failure (SLR as low as 0.251) and harness adherence failure (HFR as low as 0.142).

sources.text_snippet: Observation 2: Weak-tier models derive low Δ_benefit due to two failure modes
SLR, HFR, LPR per model

C7.2.text: Activation and adherence are separable capabilities: Qwen3-235B has SLR=0.961 but HFR=0.350.

sources.text_snippet: Loading the harness is not sufficient for benefiting from it

C7.3.text: Adherence decays over trajectory for weak models: Qwen3-32B drifts -0.39 from load to final turn vs -0.09 for Opus 4.6.

sources.text_snippet: Per-phase adherence scores
Diagnosis: Weak instruction following over long-horizon execution

C8.1.text: Missing baselines (random harness, human-written harness) weaken the claim that updating is 'flat' rather than 'uniformly weak.'

sources.text_snippet: Experimental setup section
Observation 2 discussion

C8.2.text: HFR judge pipeline may contain judge-model bias despite blinding; no cross-judge agreement analysis.

sources.text_snippet: HFR pipeline details, single judge (Sonnet 4.6)

C9.1.text: Paper honestly acknowledges its scope limitations regarding parametric fine-tuning, exhaustive model coverage, and broad generalization.

sources.text_snippet: Limitations section
